name: The Grand Budapest Hotel — Socially-Rich Naturalistic Movie fMRI
description: fMRI from 25 participants who watched a ~50-minute segment of Wes Anderson's 'The Grand Budapest Hotel', a film deliberately chosen for its dense social content — many characters, interactions, emotions, and shifting mental states. An openly available BIDS dataset well suited to studying face and person perception, theory of mind, and social-event understanding during naturalistic viewing.
citation: Visconti di Oleggio Castello M, Chauhan V, Jiahui G, Gobbini MI (2020). An fMRI dataset in response to 'The Grand Budapest Hotel', a socially-rich, naturalistic movie. Scientific Data, 7, 383.
